From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Chilean singer-songwriter

| Chinoy | |
| --- | --- |
|  | |
| Background information | |
| Born | Mauricio Castillo Moya  1982 (age 43–44) |
| Origin | [San Antonio, Chile](/wiki/San_Antonio,_Chile "San Antonio, Chile") |
| Genres | [Folk](/wiki/Folk_music "Folk music"), [trova](/wiki/Trova "Trova"), [indie folk](/wiki/Indie_folk "Indie folk") |
| Years active | 2002 – present |
| Labels | * Oveja Negra * [Quemasucabeza](/wiki/Quemasucabeza "Quemasucabeza") * Música Del Sur * Sudamerican Records * Fonarte Latino * Calaverita Records |
|  | |
| Website | [www.myspace.com/chinoysite](http://www.myspace.com/chinoysite) |

Musical artist

**Mauricio Castillo Moya**  (born 1982), known as **Chinoy**, is a Chilean singer-songwriter who has released two solo albums. Chinoy's androgynous voice, lyrics, poetics and iconic guitar technique have been compared to those of Bob Dylan by the Chilean media[[1]](#cite_note-1) and his style has been described as [trova](/wiki/Trova "Trova"), [indie folk](/wiki/Indie_folk "Indie folk") and [folk punk](/wiki/Folk_punk "Folk punk").[[2]](#cite_note-2)

## Early life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Chinoy_(musician)&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Early life")]

Chinoy was born in [San Antonio](/wiki/San_Antonio,_Chile "San Antonio, Chile"), a [commune](/wiki/Communes_of_Chile "Communes of Chile") of [Valparaíso](/wiki/Valpara%C3%ADso "Valparaíso"), Chile, where he founded the punk band Don Nadie and opened for the Argentine punk band Loquero in 2005. Loquero then invited him to spend a season in [La Plata](/wiki/La_Plata "La Plata"), [Argentina](/wiki/Argentina "Argentina"). In 2007, he moved to Valparaiso where he studied for a short time and performed in local bars. It was there that [Manuel García](/wiki/Manuel_Garc%C3%ADa_(singer-songwriter) "Manuel García (singer-songwriter)") saw him perform and invited Chinoy to open for him in Valparaiso and [Santiago](/wiki/Santiago "Santiago"). During 2007, Chinoy performed at [Concepción](/wiki/Concepci%C3%B3n_(Chile) "Concepción (Chile)"), [Puerto Montt](/wiki/Puerto_Montt "Puerto Montt"), [Punta Arenas](/wiki/Punta_Arenas "Punta Arenas"), Valparaíso and Santiago and his song *Carne de gallina* appeared on the compilation album *Escuelas de Rock 1er disco bicentenario*[[3]](#cite_note-3) (2007).[[4]](#cite_note-4)

## Musical career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Chinoy_(musician)&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Musical career")]

In 2008, Chinoy came to the attention of award-winning filmmaker [Andrés Wood](/wiki/Andr%C3%A9s_Wood "Andrés Wood"), who asked him to write the theme song for his movie [*La Buena Vida*](/wiki/The_Good_Life_(2008_film) "The Good Life (2008 film)").[[5]](#cite_note-5)

In 2009, he released his debut full-length album, *Que salgan los dragones*, on the record label [Quemasucabeza](/wiki/Quemasucabeza "Quemasucabeza").[[6]](#cite_note-6) In October 2010, he began a national tour of Chile taking in Santiago, [Talca](/wiki/Talca "Talca"), [Chillán](/wiki/Chill%C3%A1n "Chillán") and [Concepción](/wiki/Concepci%C3%B3n,_Chile "Concepción, Chile").[[7]](#cite_note-7) In January 2012, he performed at the opening ceremony of Chile's Viña del Mar International Book Festival[[8]](#cite_note-8) and by January 2013, his MySpace page had more than 800,000 views.[[9]](#cite_note-9)

## Discography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Chinoy_(musician)&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Discography")]

* 2009 - *Que salgan los dragones*
* 2010 - *Chinoy en Bogotá*

### Soundtracks

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Chinoy_(musician)&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Soundtracks")]

* 2008 - *La Buena Vida*[[10]](#cite_note-10)
